definition: The Fed 'AI put' is structurally impaired relative to 1998-2001 precedent. Current inflation at 2.5-3.5% limits easing to 150-250bp within 6 months (vs 550bp in 2001). The Fed faces a trilemma: intervene in Treasuries (fiscal dominance), cut rates (unanchor inflation), or stand pat (deleveraging cascade). The basis trade could impair Treasury transmission.
evidence: Current inflation 2.5-3.5% vs less than 2% in 2000-2001
Easing capacity: 150-250bp in 6 months vs 550bp in 2001
Average easing/tightening asymmetry ratio: 3.5x across 3 episodes
2020 precedent misleading: Fed printed into deflationary shock; AI bust with 3%+ inflation has no analogue
open_questions: Whether inflation would decelerate enough during an AI bust to widen easing capacity
How basis trade coupling would interact with Fed Treasury operations
